500+ Resources Listed

Community Resource Hub

Find non-profits, support services, health programs, and opportunities — all verified, all free to access.

15 Resources Listed 6 Categories All Verified
Scroll

Showing 15 of 15 resources

●Education

Scholarship Discovery Program

Find and apply for local and national scholarships. Free guidance on application essays, financial aid, and deadline tracking for college-bound students.

Location: Bothell Community Center, 9919 NE 180th StPhone: (555) 234-5678Hours: Mon–Fri 9AM–6PM, Sat 10AM–2PMEmail: scholarships@communityconnect.org
●Education

After-School Tutoring Network

Free K–12 tutoring in math, science, reading, and writing. Volunteer tutors available weekdays 3–6PM at multiple community centers.

Location: Various Bothell sites — call for locationPhone: (555) 234-5679Hours: Mon–Fri 3PM–6PMEmail: tutoring@communityconnect.org
●Career

Youth Career Internship Portal

Connect high school and college students with paid internships at local businesses. Earn real-world experience and college credit while building your resume.

Location: Bothell Workforce Dev. Center, 18411 NE Woodinville-Duvall RdPhone: (555) 345-6789Hours: Mon–Fri 9AM–5PMEmail: internships@communityconnect.org
●Career

Job Search Workshop Series

Monthly workshops covering resume building, interview techniques, LinkedIn, and networking. Free and open to all community members.

Location: Bothell Regional Library, 18215 98th Ave NEPhone: (555) 345-6790Hours: 2nd & 4th Tuesday 6PM–8PMEmail: workshops@communityconnect.org
●Health

Community Health Clinic

Affordable medical and dental services with sliding-scale fees. Walk-in hours Monday–Friday 9AM–5PM. No insurance required, multilingual staff.

Location: EvergreenHealth Bothell, 10010 NE 195th StPhone: (555) 456-7890Hours: Mon–Fri 9AM–5PM, Walk-ins welcomeEmail: clinic@healthcenter.org
●Health

Mental Wellness Support Line

24/7 confidential crisis support and counseling. Connect with trained professionals for mental health support, stress management, and emotional guidance.

Location: Confidential — phone & online supportPhone: (555) 911-HELPHours: 24/7 — Call anytimeEmail: support@mentalhealthline.org
●Volunteering

Neighborhood Cleanup Initiative

Join monthly community cleanups in local parks. Tools and supplies provided. Volunteers of all ages welcome to help beautify our community.

Location: Bothell Landing Park, 9919 NE 180th StPhone: (555) 567-8901Hours: 1st Saturday monthly 9AM–12PMEmail: cleanup@communityconnect.org
●Volunteering

Senior Companion Program

Match with local seniors for weekly visits, companionship, and light assistance. Training provided. Make a meaningful difference through friendship.

Location: Training at Senior Center, 234 Elder StPhone: (555) 567-8902Hours: Flexible — match your scheduleEmail: companions@seniorcare.org
●Community Projects

Community Garden Collective

Rent a plot and grow your own vegetables. Workshops on sustainable gardening, composting, and organic growing. Tools shared, water included.

Location: Community Garden, Corner of 5th & MaplePhone: (555) 678-9012Hours: Open daily dawn to duskEmail: garden@greengrow.org
●Community Projects

Little Free Library Network

Build and maintain Little Free Libraries in neighborhoods. Free books for all ages, woodworking workshops, and library setup support.

Location: Bothell Makerspace, 10810 NE 195th StPhone: (555) 678-9013Hours: Workshop 2nd Sat 10AM–2PMEmail: books@littlefreelibrary.org
●Events

Monthly Town Hall Meetings

Monthly forums to discuss community issues, meet local leaders, and voice your concerns. First Thursday of every month, 7PM at City Hall.

Location: City Hall Council Chambers, 100 Civic PlazaPhone: (555) 789-0123Hours: 1st Thursday monthly 7PM–9PMEmail: townhall@citygovernment.gov
●Events

Community Resource Fair

Annual event connecting residents with local services, nonprofits, health providers, and educational resources. Free health screenings, food, and kids activities.

Location: Central Park Main FieldPhone: (555) 789-0124Hours: Annual — 3rd Sat in Sept 10AM–4PMEmail: fair@communityconnect.org
●Career

Financial Literacy Workshops

Learn budgeting, saving, investing, and credit management. Free evening workshops in English and Spanish. Materials provided.

Location: Credit Union Community Room, 890 Bank StPhone: (555) 890-1234Hours: Wednesdays 6PM–8PMEmail: finance@moneywise.org
●Education

Youth Mentorship Network

Pair young people ages 12–18 with vetted adult mentors for guidance, academic support, and personal development. Background checks required. Open year-round.

Location: Youth Services Office, 345 Hope BlvdPhone: (555) 901-2345Hours: Match meetings flexibleEmail: mentors@youthprogram.org
●Health

Fitness in the Park

Free outdoor fitness classes every weekend! Yoga, Zumba, bootcamp, and tai chi for all fitness levels. Bring a mat and water bottle.

Location: Riverside Park PavilionPhone: (555) 012-3456Hours: Sat–Sun 8AM, 10AM, 4PMEmail: fitness@parksprogram.org
CommunityConnect

Building stronger communities through collaboration, service, and shared resources.
